github_project.title: louke-0.14.0
github_project.rationale: Active gh token belongs to `quantclaws` (`gh auth status`); the repository lives at `zillionare/louke`. Per Louke spec 1 contracts (FR-0200 / FR-1800 / NFR-0100), Project ownership is not required to match the repository owner; Project is bound to the gh user identity that drives the action. The historical `{owner}` placeholder in scout.py / wiki / older specs is template text and not a v0.14 product contract.
user_directed_corrections_2026_07_18: There is no v0.14 contract requiring Project to live under the repository owner namespace.

Project MUST be created under the current gh auth login (== `quantclaws` here), with explicit repo linking by the Runtime and an optional collaborator invite. This matches Louke's check_identity.py L6 collaboration modes.

Future Runtime code must derive gh user from `gh auth status`, derive repo owner from `git remote get-url origin`, and create the Project under the gh user. Louke final-user deployments use these same derivations; no static owner name is hardcoded.
github_project.platform_constraint_noted: GitHub Projects v2 linkProjectV2ToRepository requires Project owner to match the linked repository owner. Cross-owner link is rejected by GraphQL with VALIDATION 'Only projects owned by the same owner as the repository can be linked.' This is a platform limitation independent of Louke's contract; downstream linking requires running Project actions under the repository owner account.

github_issues.plan: Per FR-1800 contract bound to bound_spec_digest sha256:a627a43b7ad1f2834b14cebb8c8f78af949676722e9319059d02bd0e7426f596 and doc_index_digest sha256:384084747a9a67bca6eba544711da7e8de2e3a65883d41fa99ed081019ca528b. 1 issue per FR/NFR (21 FR + 3 NFR = 24 issues) under v0.14-001 workflow reflow spec. Title prefix '[FR-XXXX]' / '[NFR-XXXX]'.
